title: Lenny's Newsletter — product, growth, careers
description: Lenny Rachitsky (ex-Airbnb) writes the deepest essays on product sense, growth, prioritization, and PM careers. Many free deep-dives across the topics PrepOS drills on.

title: SVPG — articles by Marty Cagan and team
description: The canonical product-discovery frame. Read these to understand what 'good product' actually looks like — the lens every senior interviewer is implicitly using.

title: Marty Cagan — The Product Manager
description: Cagan's definition of the PM role. Useful frame for answering 'what makes a great PM' and similar behavioral / strategy openers.

title: First Round Review — operator essays
description: Long-form operator essays from PMs, founders, and engineering leaders. Strong on metrics, prioritization, and leadership stories — the kind of structured retrospectives that make great behavioral answers.

title: Reforge — growth, retention, experimentation
description: Working-PM education on growth loops, retention, and experimentation. Direct fit for execution and analytics rounds.

title: Mind the Product — PM articles and talks
description: Community-driven PM articles and conference talks. Strongest on product discovery, PM career, and cross-functional collaboration.

title: Andrew Chen — growth, metrics, marketplaces
description: a16z general partner; ex-Uber growth lead. Canonical essays on growth loops, network effects, and marketplace metrics — the math behind execution-round questions.

title: Sachin Rekhi — PM frameworks blog
description: Ex-LinkedIn group PM. Short, well-named frameworks (e.g., problem statement design, PM-EM disagreement) that map cleanly to interview answers.

title: Julie Zhuo — The Looking Glass
description: Ex-Facebook design VP. Leadership, taste, and giving-feedback essays — high-credibility source material for behavioral stories.

title: John Cutler — The Beautiful Mess
description: Systems-thinking essays on product orgs, prioritization, and PM-eng collaboration. Useful for technical-collaboration and prioritization rounds.

title: a16z — strategy, AI, and market essays
description: Strategy and AI product takes from the a16z partner pool. Useful for strategy-round framing (market timing, durable advantage) and AI PM concept depth.

title: Anthropic — building with Claude
description: Canonical reference for evals, safety, tool use, and prompt design. The vocabulary an AI PM is expected to use in interviews on AI product judgment.

title: OpenAI Cookbook — evals and patterns
description: Worked examples for evals, retrieval, structured outputs, and latency-cost tradeoffs. Concrete material to reference when an AI PM round asks 'how would you measure this'.

title: Harvard Business Review — strategy and leadership
description: Evergreen strategy and leadership essays (Porter on competition, classic frameworks). Senior interviewers expect candidates to be conversant in this vocabulary.

title: Stratechery — Ben Thompson on strategy
description: Aggregation theory and platform-strategy framing. Most posts are paywalled but the free interviews and concept primers are foundational for strategy-round answers.

title: Shreyas Doshi — high-agency PM essays
description: Ex-Stripe / Twitter / Google PM. Sharp, opinionated essays on prioritization, taste, and operating at staff/principal level. Useful for behavioral and prioritization rounds.

title: PMExercises — community answers to PM questions
description: Open question bank with community-written answers. Useful for seeing how others structure responses to canonical PM prompts — compare against your own.

title: The PM Interview — question hub
description: Browse PM interview questions by company and round.
